description: Full modernization rewrite of rustRoast coffee roaster control system. Restructure Rust backend into modules, replace React frontend with SvelteKit, add API auth, shared telemetry schema, docker-compose dev environment, and CI pipeline. Building toward Artisan Roaster Scope replacement.

userStories.title: Remove legacy coffee-roaster-bridge crate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the legacy coffee-roaster-bridge removed so new contributors aren't confused by duplicate functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: coffee-roaster-bridge/ directory is deleted
Root Cargo.toml workspace members no longer reference coffee-roaster-bridge
cargo build succeeds for remaining workspace members
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create backend module skeleton and AppError type
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the backend module structure created with a unified error type so subsequent extraction stories have a place to land.
acceptanceCriteria: Directory structure created: routes/, services/, db/, ws/ under crates/rustroast-server/src/
Each directory has a mod.rs with placeholder module declarations
error.rs created with AppError enum (NotFound, Validation, MqttUnavailable, MqttTimeout, Database, Internal) implementing IntoResponse
config.rs created with typed Config struct loaded from environment variables (mqtt_host, mqtt_port, db_path, api_key, static_dir, web_port)
main.rs imports the new modules (existing code still in main.rs is fine)
cargo build succeeds with no warnings
cargo clippy passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract DB models into db module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all database structs and models in a dedicated db module.
acceptanceCriteria: db/models.rs contains all domain structs: RoastSession, SessionTelemetry, RoastProfile, ProfilePoint, RoastEvent, DeviceInfo, and their Create/Update variants
db/mod.rs re-exports all model types
main.rs and services.rs import models from db module instead of defining them locally
cargo build succeeds with no warnings
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract session and event service

acceptanceCriteria: services/session.rs contains RoastSessionService with all session methods: create, list, get, update, start, pause, resume, complete, delete, get_with_telemetry, add_telemetry_point, get_session_telemetry, get_active_session
services/session.rs also contains roast event methods: create_roast_event, get_roast_events, update_roast_event, delete_roast_event
services/mod.rs re-exports the service
main.rs route handlers call the service from the new location
All existing session and event API endpoints work identically
cargo build succeeds with no warnings
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract profile service and Artisan parser

services/profile.rs contains the Artisan .alog parser (parse_artisan_alog and helper structs)
services/mod.rs re-exports the profile service
main.rs route handlers call the service from the new location

userStories.title: Extract MQTT consumer into service module

acceptanceCriteria: services/mqtt.rs contains the MQTT consumer loop (previously in main.rs ~lines 685-808)
services/mqtt.rs contains the MQTT publisher helper for sending control commands

acceptanceCriteria: routes/control.rs contains handlers: set_setpoint, set_fan_pwm, set_heater_pwm, set_mode, set_heater_enable, set_pid, emergency_stop, reset_mqtt

routes/sessions.rs contains handlers: create_session, list_sessions, get_session, update_session, start_session, pause_session, resume_session, complete_session, delete_session, get_session_telemetry, create_roast_event, get_roast_events

userStories.title: Extract WebSocket handlers and clean up main.rs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want WebSocket logic in a ws/ module and main.rs reduced to startup-only code.
acceptanceCriteria: ws/telemetry.rs contains the telemetry WebSocket handler and debug WebSocket handler
ws/mod.rs re-exports WebSocket handlers
main.rs contains ONLY: config loading, database init, MQTT connection, router assembly, server startup, graceful shutdown (~50-80 lines of actual logic)

userStories.title: Define typed telemetry schema in Rust
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a typed Telemetry struct that validates MQTT payloads so field mismatches are caught at deserialization time.
acceptanceCriteria: Telemetry struct in db/models.rs (or a new schema.rs) with serde field mappings matching ESP32 JSON: beanTemp, envTemp, heaterPWM, fanPWM, setpoint, mode, heaterEnabled, pidKp, pidKi, pidKd
MQTT consumer deserializes incoming telemetry into the typed struct instead of storing raw JSON strings
Unknown fields log a tracing::warn instead of silently dropping
WebSocket broadcasts the typed struct (serialized to JSON) instead of raw MQTT payload
Unit test validates deserialization of a sample ESP32 payload
Unit test validates that unknown fields produce a warning (not an error)
cargo test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add API key authentication middleware
userStories.description: As a user, I want control endpoints protected by API key so only authorized users can operate the heater and fan.
acceptanceCriteria: Tower middleware checks Authorization: Bearer <key> header on protected routes
API key configured via RUSTROAST_API_KEY environment variable
All /control/* endpoints require valid API key
Read-only endpoints (GET /sessions, GET /telemetry, GET /profiles, health checks) do NOT require auth
Missing or invalid key returns 401 Unauthorized with JSON error body
If RUSTROAST_API_KEY env var is not set, auth is disabled (development convenience)
cargo build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Configure Axum static file serving for SvelteKit SPA
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Axum to serve the SvelteKit static build so the app is a single deployment artifact.
acceptanceCriteria: tower-http ServeDir serves files from configurable directory (default: ./static, env var: RUSTROAST_STATIC_DIR)
Fallback to index.html for any non-API, non-WebSocket path (SPA client-side routing)
API routes (/api/*) and WebSocket routes (/ws/*) take priority over static serving
Cache-Control headers: immutable for hashed assets (*.js, *.css with hash), no-cache for index.html
Server starts successfully even if static directory doesn't exist (logs warning)
cargo build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create WebSocket store with typed telemetry
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Svelte store that connects to the backend WebSocket and provides typed real-time telemetry data.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/stores/telemetry.ts exports a readable Svelte store with typed telemetry data (beanTemp, envTemp, heaterPWM, fanPWM, setpoint, mode, heaterEnabled, pidKp, pidKi, pidKd)
Store connects to backend WebSocket at ws://<host>/ws/telemetry
Automatic reconnection with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s)
Connection status exported as a separate store: 'connected' | 'reconnecting' | 'disconnected'
Store parses incoming JSON and updates typed state
WebSocket URL configurable via environment variable VITE_WS_URL

userStories.title: Create mock device simulator for docker-compose
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want simulated roast telemetry so I can develop without real hardware.
acceptanceCriteria: tools/mock-device/ directory with a Python or Rust script that simulates an ESP32
Publishes telemetry to roaster/mock-device-01/telemetry at 1Hz via MQTT
Simulates 3 roast profiles: light (~10 min, FC at 196C, drop at 205C), medium (~12 min, FC at 200C, drop at 215C), dark (~15 min, FC at 200C, SC at 224C, drop at 230C)
Realistic thermal dynamics: initial charge drop, turning point, steady ramp, FC exotherm, development
Cycles through profiles continuously (one roast, 60s cooldown, next roast)
Subscribes to control topics and logs received commands
Dockerfile for the mock device
mock-device service added to docker-compose.yml
docker-compose up starts all three services and dashboard shows simulated data
Typecheck passes
